name: Web Directives: Virtual Elements
summary: A control:for-each / control:if directive expressed as a real HTML comment marker, upgraded by the customComments registry (#1131) with its options parsed by the default namespaced parser (#1132). The markers are virtual elements — they carry logic but add no node to the layout tree, so a 3-column CSS Grid keeps its direct parent-child relationship whether the conditional card shows or hides.
description:
Concept

Web Directives express structural control flow (control:if, control:for-each) as real HTML comment markers rather than wrapper elements. The customComments registry (#1131) upgrade()s the page's comment nodes onto CustomComment (#1130) subclasses and runs their lifecycle; the shipped default parser (#1132) turns each marker's text (control:if when="showMiddle") into { name, options }.

The virtual-element claim

Because the markers are comments, they add zero element nodes to the layout tree. The case-study grid's grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr) stays intact whether the conditional card is shown or hidden — the exact thing a framework <If> wrapper element breaks. A live readout shows the grid's element-child count excludes the markers.

Scope

One registry, two directives, one upgrade() walk; each directive re-parses its own comment text through the shared default parser. Native DOM (a plug demo, not block rendering).
